title: SACRAMENTO —

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "On February 15, 2023, an FDA panel recommended that naloxone be approved for over-the-counter (OTC) sales.",
    "Public health experts warned that naloxone's high price point would limit access.",
    "Assemblymember Ortega introduced AB 1060 to cap out-of-pocket costs for OTC naloxone at $10 by requiring MediCal and private insurers to cover the drug.",
    "Governor Newsom announced that the CalRX program would include a cheaper naloxone nasal spray.",
    "CalRX would have a contract in place to provide low-cost naloxone to Californians by this summer.",
    "Naloxone is a safe and easy-to-administer drug that can reverse a fentanyl overdose within minutes.",
    "The focus on expanding access to naloxone was spurred by a sharp increase in fentanyl and synthetic opioid overdoses.",
    "US overdose deaths exceeded 100,000 in 2021.",
    "Prior to the political efforts, drugstores began selling naloxone over the counter at $44.99."
  ]
}
```
